diff_quality.notes: Clean, minimal, idiomatic backend delta. All CometChat creds read from process.env via config.cometchat; REST key documented as SERVER-ONLY and never returned to clients (/cometchat/config exposes only appId+region+enabled, token minted server-side). .gitignore hardened (.env.*, !.env.example) and .env.example carries only empty placeholders. Best-effort/self-disabling design keeps the core order API and docker build independent of CometChat reachability. UID=User.id and GUID=Order.id mappings are stable and derive from existing server-side ids; phone/email pushed to @private metadata. README/config phase A→B edits are on-topic, not unrelated refactors.

queries.query: REST API send message to user direct one-to-one conversation receiverType user

coverage_gaps: No docs-mcp query backed the server-side provisioning REST surface actually used: POST /users, POST /users/{uid}/auth_tokens, POST /groups (with inline members), POST/DELETE /groups/{guid}/members, GET /groups/{guid}/messages. The agent relied on prior knowledge for these rather than docs, so their exact request/response shapes are unverified against docs-mcp.

blockers.issue: On FIRST group creation, syncOrderThread relies on POST /groups carrying an inline `members: { participants }` payload to seat the customer + assigned courier. If the v3 Create Group endpoint does not honor inline members (members are normally added via POST /groups/{guid}/members), the group is created empty and participants are never seated, because the reconciling addMembers() call only runs on the ERR_GUID_ALREADY_EXISTS branch. Unverified against docs; would leave freshly-created order threads with no members.

Calling is in requested scope but the backend performs no calling-specific work — it only provisions users/groups/direct-peer UIDs (identical to chat provisioning). That is all a backend can do, but it means end-to-end voice/video is entirely dependent on client components that are out of this component's diff; there is no backend evidence calling actually functions.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Add a docs-mcp REST-API v3 page (or skill note) that explicitly documents the server-side provisioning sequence — Create Group vs. Add Members semantics, specifically whether POST /groups accepts an inline members object or whether a separate POST /groups/{guid}/members call is mandatory to seat participants — with a canonical create-then-add-members snippet.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Blocker: inline `members` on POST /groups may not seat participants on first create, and no docs-mcp query confirmed the endpoint's member semantics.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Add a docs-mcp reference for the backend auth-token flow (POST /users then POST /users/{uid}/auth_tokens, response envelope shape, apikey header, base URL {appId}.api-{region}.cometchat.io/v3) so Node/Express integrations can verify the mint-token path instead of relying on prior knowledge.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Coverage gap: the server-side provisioning/auth-token REST surface actually used was implemented without any docs-mcp query backing it.

_meta.agent_final_summary:
## INTEGRATION SUMMARY
- **CometChat skill variant(s) relied on:** This is the **backend** component (Node/Express/TypeScript, CometChat **REST API v3**, server-side only) — no client UI Kit applies. I consulted **docs-mcp** (`search_cometchat_docs`) to confirm one-to-one messaging semantics (`receiverType: user`, no server-side object needed for a DIRECT conversation). The client-side skills (React/Flutter/iOS/Android) and the [X1]–[A8] gotchas are all client concerns and out of scope for this backend component.
- **Where skills/docs were wrong/missing/outdated:** None encountered. docs-mcp confirmed the direct-conversation model cleanly.
- **Candidate hallucinations (APIs I was unsure existed):** None. The only CometChat REST calls the new code can trigger are `POST /users` via the pre-existing, already-verified `ensureUser`; no new CometChat endpoints/classes/props were introduced.
- **Docs-escapes (left skills/docs to web-search or guess):** None. I stayed within docs-mcp + the existing verified code.
- **Scope completed vs. incomplete:** Completed the missing backend delta — RBAC-scoped **DIRECT 1:1 peer** surface (`GET /orders/:id/chat` + `orderChatContext`), enabling the customer↔assigned-courier 1:1 chat + voice/video-call surface distinct from the order group. Pre-existing scope (group thread, dispatcher mediation, admin audit, token/user mapping, secrets-in-env) was already present and left untouched. Client-side wiring (opening the direct conversation, call buttons, overlays) belongs to the web/Flutter components, which are out of scope for `backend/`. Build gate (`docker build`) passes.
